clipd — quick start (friends beta)
==================================

clipd keeps the last couple of minutes of your screen in memory and writes
them to a video file the instant you press a hotkey — so you can save a clip
AFTER something cool happens. It can also record straight to disk on demand.

Nothing is uploaded anywhere. Everything stays on your PC.


1. RUN IT
---------
Double-click  clipd.exe .

It runs in the system tray (the little icons by the clock — click the "^"
to find it). There is no main window; right-click the tray icon for the menu
(Save clip, Start/stop recording, Settings…, Open clips folder, Quit).

FIRST-RUN WARNING (this is expected):
Windows SmartScreen may say "Windows protected your PC" because this build is
not code-signed (that comes later). Click  More info  →  Run anyway .
It is the same clipd.exe you downloaded.


2. THE HOTKEYS (work system-wide, even in games)
------------------------------------------------
  Ctrl + Alt + S      Save the last ~2 minutes as a clip
  Ctrl + Alt + F9     Start / stop recording to disk

Rebind them in Settings → Hotkeys (press the new combo), or in the config file.


3. WHERE YOUR CLIPS GO
----------------------
By default, clips land in clipd's working folder (where clipd.exe is), named
  clipd_<timestamp>.mp4
Change it in Settings → Output folder (or set  dir  in the config file).
Tray → "Open clips folder" opens it; Settings → Recent clips lists the last 20.


4. SETTINGS
-----------
Tray → "Settings…" opens a window to change quality, resolution, fps, buffer
length, output folder, audio, and hotkeys. Most changes save to the config and
apply on the NEXT start (the window tells you which need a restart).

Config file:  %APPDATA%\clipd\config.toml
A commented template is included as  config.template.toml  — copy it there to
start from, or just edit settings in the window (it writes the same file).


5. IF SOMETHING GOES WRONG
--------------------------
There's a log at:  %LOCALAPPDATA%\clipd\logs\clipd.log.<date>
It records every save, device change, and warning — check it first (or send it
along with a bug report). "Quit" from the tray shuts everything down cleanly.


Thanks for testing! Your machine + GPU is exactly the kind of coverage this
beta needs — please report what worked, what didn't, and your hardware.
